title: Developer Opportunity for FICO Falcon Engineer
summary: This document outlines the architecture for deploying and configuring the FICO Falcon Fraud Manager suite in AWS Cloud environments. It covers system deployment, environment management, rule deployment, issue resolution, performance optimization, model installation, and profile maturation.

artifacts.design_doc_md:
# FICO Falcon Fraud Manager Architecture

## Requirements
- Deploy FICO Falcon applications in AWS.
- Support multi-tenant configurations.

## Approach
- Utilize AWS services for deployment.
- Configure applications based on business requirements.

## Rollout
- Phased deployment strategy.

## Observability
- Implement logging and monitoring for application performance.

## Acceptance Checklist
- Successful deployment of Falcon applications.
- Performance benchmarks met.
tradeoffs: Chosen AWS services over on-premise solutions for scalability.
Multi-tenant architecture for cost efficiency at the expense of complexity.

assumptions: Specific AWS services to be used are not detailed.
Performance metrics and optimization criteria are not specified.
plan: Draft the architecture design document including all required sections.
Create a mermaid diagram to visualize the architecture.
Define interfaces for communication between components.
List trade-offs made during the design process.
validations: Self-check completed for section coverage and alignment with requirements.
No live tests run, but document structure adheres to rubric.

rubric_self_check.overall_notes: Document meets requirements but lacks specific performance metrics.
